p00_b00: Dr. Yong-Hyun Chae, Director of Yonsei Saebom Medical Clinic
p01_b01: YONG-HYUN CHAE (DR. CHAE) DOCTOR & DIRECTOR OF YONSEI SAEBOM CLINIC.
p03_b00: Did you know there is a specific window of time in which a child can grow?
p03_b01: The timing and duration of treatment depend on the child's growth pattern, target height, and the progression of secondary sexual characteristics. However, if the timing is too late, it becomes difficult for a child to grow sufficiently even with growth treatment. We therefore recommend having an evaluation done at the appropriate time. Because the evaluation is separate from treatment, you can have it done without any significant burden.

p08_b01: Why is a height-growth program such an essential priority?
p09_b00: Appropriate height growth is evidence that a child is developing in good health.
p09_b01: According to Fortune 500 data, the average height of Fortune 500 CEOs is 6 feet 0 inches (183 cm) — about 2.5 inches (6.4 cm) taller than the average American man.
p09_b03: Bestselling author and speaker Malcolm Gladwell notes that “most of us, in ways that we are not entirely aware of, automatically associate leadership ability with imposing physical stature,” calling it a kind of “unconscious bias.”
p09_b05: Height-growth treatment is one way to open up more opportunities for a child.
p11_b00: When a child grows less than 4 cm (1.6 inches) in a year. When a child is among the 10 shortest boys or girls in their class.
p11_b01: When a child's growth slows down or suddenly stops. When a child is more than 10 cm (4 inches) shorter than average.
p11_b05: A child who dreams of becoming an athlete, supermodel, or entertainer. When there is an abnormality in a child's growth hormone levels.
p12_b01: What does the treatment process look like?
p13_b03: Complete a medical questionnaire. Bone age and full-body X-ray examination. Measurement of height, weight, and body composition analysis.
p13_b07: Additional tests: blood and urine · Prescription: individualized hormones · Once-monthly online consultation: measurement
p13_b08: Testing (essential tests), heavy-metal balance, customized nutrition, height·weight·body composition measurement. Hair mineral analysis, precocious puberty (additional testing). Exercise, posture correction, hormones, customized exercise, posture correction, and nutrition prescription management.
p13_b14: Continued care for 6 months to 1 year after treatment ends
p14_b03: Sex hormone, thyroid hormone, and stress management
p15_b01: A track record of success with almost no failures – on average,
p15_b05: 9 cm beyond their predicted adult height.
p15_b08: Over 100,000 treatment cases over the past 10 years
p15_b13: Sleep management, weight control, nutrition, posture and balance management,
p15_b15: and integrated care extending all the way to puberty management.
p15_b19: A differentiated solution that manages growth, puberty, obesity, nutrition, posture, physical fitness, and even adolescent health conditions.
p15_b20: Close follow-up monitoring. Physical examinations. Regular blood tests.
p15_b21: -> Proven to be a safe treatment
p17_b00: Phase 4 clinical trials have demonstrated that there are no serious side effects when growth hormone is administered to patients.
p17_b01: Some children may experience mild side effects while using growth hormone, but these naturally disappear over time.
p17_b02: Known side effects include muscle and joint pain (which resolves over time), swelling (which subsides naturally), hypothyroidism (extremely rare), and headaches caused by increased intracranial pressure (symptoms disappear immediately once treatment is stopped).
p17_b03: We monitor for side effects through continuous, regular check-ups.
p18_b03: 1. When is the best time to start?
p18_b04: In pediatric growth treatment, timing has the greatest impact on results. Generally, the best window is ages 8–12 for girls and 9–13 for boys, though this can vary from child to child. Whether treatment is possible and how effective it will be can be confirmed through a consultation.
p18_b06: Mild side effects (joint pain, swelling, headache, etc.) may appear early on, but they usually resolve by briefly pausing treatment. We repeat tests every 6 months to check for side effects, and through many years of treatment experience it has been confirmed that there are no side effects that persist into adulthood.
p18_b09: Treatment costs are billed on an annual basis. Generally, 2–3 years of treatment are needed. The cost includes growth hormone management, puberty-progression management, sleep management, growth nutrition management, and corrective-exercise coaching.
p19_b01: 4. How much will my child grow after treatment?
p19_b02: It depends on the duration of treatment. On average, about 3 years of treatment can yield roughly 8 cm of additional growth, and the effect varies depending on genetic factors, sex, lifestyle habits, and whether precocious puberty is present.
p19_b04: 5. How is the success of treatment evaluated?
p19_b05: Every 6 months, X-rays are used to confirm how much more the child has matured compared to the average child of the same age (one who has not received treatment).
p19_b07: Several years of treatment are required. Typically, with 2–3 years of treatment a child gets close to their target height. However, this can vary somewhat depending on the timing of treatment, sex, stage of pubertal development, bone age, and other factors.
p19_b08: 7. What kind of treatment is needed?
p19_b09: We provide integrated management of every aspect related to growth, as follows.
p19_b10b: Balance of hormones that affect growth
p19_b10f: Height-growth exercise and body-axis corrective exercise
